item.title: Mortality trends from colorectal pathologies in the United States (1999-2020): a retrospective cohort study using CDC WONDER.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Colorectal diseases encompass both malignant and benign conditions with significant public health implications. While colorectal cancer (CRC) mortality has been well characterized, population-level...

item.title: Diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease following symptomatic faecal immunochemical testing - A population based cohort study.
item.description: The British journal of surgery - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and colorectal cancer (CRC) share overlapping symptoms. Faecal Immunochemical Test (FIT) is mandated in UK primary care to triage symptomatic patients suspected of ...

item.title: Discordant Clostridioides difficile testing as a predictor of inflammatory bowel disease therapy escalation.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - In patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), Clostridioides difficile infection (CDI) presents with clinical features indistinguishable from the underlying disease. Sequential polymerase chai...

item.title: Exposure to per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances and inflammatory bowel disease: review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of exposure science & environmental epidemiology - Review - Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are stable chemicals that are well preserved in the environment and persist in the human body. PFAS have been associated with inflammatory bowel disease (...

item.title: Risk factors for immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis: a retrospective multi-center cohort study using electronic health records.
item.description: Cancer immunology, immunotherapy : CII - Multicenter study - Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) are effective for many cancers but often cause immune-related adverse events, particularly gastrointestinal (GI) complications such as colitis. Identifying risk ...

item.title: Development and evaluation of a sham diet intended as a placebo-control diet for food-based interventional trials in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Journal of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics - The 'gold standard' for successful blinding in controlled food-based dietary interventional trials includes a sham diet. This study aimed to develop and evaluate a sham diet intended for use as a c...

item.title: Outcomes of Percutaneous Coronary Intervention in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), comprising Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), has been associated with elevated cardiovascular risks. However, the impact of IBD on outc...

item.title: Associations between oral health problems and inflammatory bowel disease: evidence from prospective cohort study.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - An association exists between oral health problems and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, few studies have considered comprehensive oral health problems and the concurrent presence of multi...
